description:
Safe `--max-concurrency > 1` via OS-process sharding. This is the REAL fix for CF2; CF2a (mutagen-33b.2) only gates the hazard. Decision recorded below — supersedes the original "(a) vs (b)" fork.

## Why sharding, and why NOT in-VM partitioning
The binding constraint is process-GLOBAL test-runner state, confirmed in code:
  - mutation_loop.ex:52-55,196-206 — every mutant runs ExUnit.run/0 against the ONE named ExUnit.Server singleton (keyed on TEST modules).
  - mutation_runner.ex:406-416 — loaded binaries are cover-instrumented; :cover counters are shared, live through the mutation phase.
ExUnit has no supported multi-session-per-VM mode, so correct concurrency REQUIRES one BEAM per concurrent worker.

  - REJECTED (a) in-VM module-partition: groups sites by SOURCE module, but neither ExUnit.Server nor :cover is keyed on the source module — two groups still collide on the one server + cover counters. Removes only the code-server collision, leaves the dominant ones. Low ceiling too (parallelizes cheap compile, must serialize the expensive N*T test run).
  - RESERVED (b1) worker-node pool (warm Erlang nodes, ship mutants, per-node restore): max performance, max complexity. Only pursue if (c) per-shard setup cost is proven to dominate.

## Approach (c)
Parent deterministically partitions the S sites into K shards (K ~ cores), runs K `mix mutagen` SUBPROCESSES each over its shard, merges the JSON reports. Each shard runs the byte-identical v1.0 serial engine UNCHANGED — isolation by construction (separate OS process = its own ExUnit.Server / :cover / code server).

Two synergies that make this cheaper than it looks:
  1. Compute coverage + the F2 line->{test module} index ONCE in the parent and pass to shards (serialized file / flag), so shards skip redundant baseline+coverage. (This is why this ticket is blocked by F2b.)
  2. The existing System.cmd child-project integration harness already boots child mix runs — reuse that machinery.

## Flag semantics
Keep the in-VM async_stream path serial-only (concurrency 1, the safe default). Parallelism becomes shard count: repurpose --max-concurrency (or add --shards) to mean "number of OS-process shards." When this lands, remove the CF2a experimental warning.

## Determinism / correctness gate
Site partition + seed distribution must be deterministic; the MERGED report must be byte-identical to a single serial run (merge by sorting on site id). Extend the byte-identity gate to assert "sharded report == serial report" on a fixture.

## Build gate (sequencing)
DECIDE-TO-BUILD only after F2 lands AND a measurement shows post-F2 serial runtime is still insufficient on a realistic suite. Record the measurement (sites, suite size, serial wall-clock) in this ticket before implementing — F2 may cut runtime enough that coarse sharding is low-urgency.

## Done
- --shards N (or --max-concurrency repurposed) runs N isolated mix mutagen shards over a deterministic site partition and merges to a byte-identical report.
- Coverage/index computed once in the parent, passed to shards (no redundant per-shard coverage).
- byte-identity test: sharded report == serial report.
- CF2a experimental warning removed (parallelism is now correct).
title: CF2b: Safe parallelism via OS-process sharding (shard sites → parallel mix mutagen → merge)
